name: Crab-Stuffed Crepes
recipeInstructions:
Crepes: Combine eggs, milk, and butter in a blender. Add flour and blend until smooth. Refrigerate batter at least 2 hours and up to 12 hours before making crepes. Cook crepes by pouring 3 tablespoons batter into a greased pan and cooking on one side only. Assemble crepes by blending half the crab filling with Swiss cheese sauce, adjusting seasoning as needed. Place a spoonful of crab mixture on each crepe and roll, placing seam side down in two buttered large rectangular glass baking dishes. Spoon remaining crab mixture over crepes and sprinkle with grated cheese and dot with butter. Bake uncovered at 400°F for 20 minutes or until hot and bubbly. This dish may be frozen or refrigerated before serving; if chilled, remove from refrigerator 30 minutes before baking.
Crab Filling: Melt butter in a skillet, stir in scallions and crabmeat. Cook lightly and add seasonings, including vermouth. Cook rapidly until liquid is almost evaporated, scraping into a bowl.
Swiss Cheese Sauce: Blend cornstarch and milk. In the same skillet used for cooking the filling, reduce vermouth to 2 tablespoons. Remove from heat and stir in cornstarch mixture. Add cream, salt, and pepper, cooking several minutes until slightly thickened. Stir in cheese and cook until melted and well blended; adjust seasoning as needed.
